key_insight: The paper unifies CutFEM for finite-strain elasticity as a single energy minimization problem, with stresses, tangents, and stabilization all derived variationally and automatically from the energy.
engineering_relevance: This gives a robust unfitted finite-element workflow for complex geometries and evolving boundaries without re-deriving constitutive equations for each hyperelastic material.
method: Build one augmented energy: bulk hyperelastic energy + Nitsche boundary terms + ghost-penalty stabilization.
Use automatic differentiation to obtain the first Piola stress and consistent tangent directly from the scalar energy density.
Analyze the linearized Newton step to prove cut-independent stability and quasi-optimal convergence.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal h-convergence for linear, quadratic, and cubic elements on a smooth benchmark, plus improved rates under local mesh refinement near corner singularities.
watch_item: Accuracy can still be limited at mixed Dirichlet–Neumann junctions by corner singularities unless local refinement is used.
paper_title: A Unified CutFEM Formulation for Finite-Strain Elasticity: Energy Minimisation and Corner Singularities
